title: Adding Locales
content: docs-template ships with English (en) and Thai (th). This page explains how to add a new locale to the site.

id: Adding Locales > Steps > 1. Register the locale in routing config#p0
title: 1. Register the locale in routing config
content: Add the new locale identifier to the locales array in src/i18n/routing.ts. Optionally update defaultLocale:

id: Adding Locales > Steps > 2. Create a messages file#p0
title: 2. Create a messages file
content: Create messages/[locale].json and add translated values for every UI string key that exists in messages/en.json:

id: Adding Locales > Steps > 2. Create a messages file#p1
title: 2. Create a messages file
content: Then replace the values in messages/ja.json with the translated strings.

id: Adding Locales > Steps > 2. Create a messages file#p2
title: 2. Create a messages file
content: Every key present in the English messages file must also exist in the new locale's file. Missing keys will cause runtime errors when users switch to that locale.

id: Adding Locales > Steps > 3. Create the docs folder and navigation#p0
title: 3. Create the docs folder and navigation
content: Create a navigation file and your MDX content for the new locale:

id: Adding Locales > Steps > 3. Create the docs folder and navigation#p1
title: 3. Create the docs folder and navigation
content: Translate the title values in src/docs/ja/navigation.json, then create the corresponding MDX files under src/docs/ja/[group]/[slug].mdx.

id: Adding Locales > Steps > 3. Create the docs folder and navigation#p2
title: 3. Create the docs folder and navigation
content: Keep the slug values identical across all locale navigation files. Only translate the title values and MDX content.

title: Result
content: After completing these steps, the new locale will be available at /ja/docs/... and will appear in the locale switcher automatically.

title: Navigation Structure
content: The sidebar is entirely driven by a JSON file — no code changes needed. This page explains the structure and how to reorder items.

title: How It Works
content: Each locale has its own navigation file:

title: How It Works
content: src/docs/en/navigation.json

title: How It Works
content: src/docs/th/navigation.json

title: How It Works
content: The sidebar renders groups and pages in the exact order they appear in the file.

title: File Structure
content: Top-level array — ordered list of sidebar groups.

title: File Structure
content: Each group has a slug, a title, and a pages array.

title: File Structure
content: Each page entry has a slug (matches the MDX filename without .mdx) and a title (displayed in the sidebar).

title: Reordering Items
content: To change the order pages appear in a group, reorder the entries in the pages array. To change the order groups appear in the sidebar, reorder the top-level objects. No other changes are required.

title: Reordering Items
content: The en and th navigation files must contain the same slug values. The sidebar structure should be identical across locales — only the title values should differ.

title: URL Pattern
content: Every page is served at:

title: URL Pattern
content: For example, { "slug": "configuration" } inside the "guides" group is available at /en/docs/guides/configuration.

title: Site Settings
content: This page covers the key files to edit when branding docs-template as your own: the site metadata, logo, and theme color tokens.

title: Site Metadata
content: Edit src/app/layout.tsx to update the page title, description, and other Open Graph metadata:

title: Logo
content: The logo rendered in the sidebar header is defined in src/components/docs/docs-logo.tsx. Replace the contents of that component with your own logo — an image, SVG, or text:

title: Logo
content: Place static assets (images, fonts) in the public/ directory so they are served at the root path.

title: Theme Colors
content: docs-template uses Tailwind CSS v4 with CSS custom properties defined in src/styles/globals.css. All design tokens live inside an @theme block.

id: Site Settings > Theme Colors > Gray Palette#p0
title: Gray Palette
content: The base neutral scale runs from --color-gray-950 (darkest) to --color-gray-0 (white). Override any stop to shift the entire site's neutral tones:

id: Site Settings > Theme Colors > Primary Colors#p0
title: Primary Colors
content: The primary color scale is mapped from the orange palette by default. Remap these five tokens to switch the accent color across the entire site:

id: Site Settings > Theme Colors > Adaptive Color Tokens#p0
title: Adaptive Color Tokens
content: Semantic tokens for background, text, and stroke each carry both a light-mode and a dark-mode value on a single line using the var(--tw-light, …) var(--tw-dark, …) syntax introduced in Tailwind CSS v4:

id: Site Settings > Theme Colors > Adaptive Color Tokens#p1
title: Adaptive Color Tokens
content: The first value (--tw-light) is active in light mode; the second (--tw-dark) activates when the .dark class is present on a parent element.

id: Site Settings > Theme Colors > Adaptive Color Tokens#p2
title: Adaptive Color Tokens
content: Point the gray step to a different palette value. For example, to make the default background slightly warmer, replace var(--color-gray-0) with a custom hex token you've defined earlier in the same @theme block.

title: Deploying
content: docs-template is a standard Next.js application and can be deployed to any platform that supports Next.js.

title: Pre-build Scripts
content: Two scripts must be considered before deploying to production.

title: Search index
content: The full-text search index is generated automatically by bun build, so no extra step is required for most deployments.

title: Git metadata
content: To display accurate last-modified dates and author information on each page, run the Git metadata script before the build:

title: Git metadata
content: generate-git-metadata also runs automatically via lint-staged on every commit, keeping metadata up to date as you work.

title: Vercel
content: Import the repository on vercel.com and deploy with zero configuration. Vercel auto-detects Next.js and sets the correct build command.

title: Vercel
content: To include Git metadata, set the build command in the Vercel dashboard:

title: Netlify
content: Use the Next.js on Netlify build plugin. Set the build command to:

title: Self-hosted
content: Run the production build on any Node.js 20+ server:

title: Self-hosted
content: The server starts on port 4000 by default.

title: Self-hosted
content: If your deployment platform clones a shallow Git history, the generate-git-metadata script may not have access to full commit history. Use git fetch --unshallow in your CI pipeline to ensure accurate metadata.

title: Installation
content: This page walks you through cloning the repository, installing dependencies, and starting the development server for the first time.

title: Prerequisites
content: Before you begin, ensure you have the following installed:

title: Prerequisites
content: Node.js 20 or higher

title: Prerequisites
content: Bun (recommended) — the project uses Bun as the package manager

title: 1. Clone the repository
content: Alternatively, click "Use this template" on the GitHub repository page to create a new repository from this template without the commit history.

id: Installation > Steps > 3. Start the development server#p0
title: 3. Start the development server
content: Open http://localhost:4000 in your browser. The development server automatically generates the search index before starting.

id: Installation > Steps > 3. Start the development server#p1
title: 3. Start the development server
content: The bun dev command runs generate-search-index automatically before starting the server. You do not need to run it manually during development.

title: Available Scripts
content: | Script | Description |

title: Available Scripts
content: | bun dev | Generate search index, then start development server on port 4000 |

title: Available Scripts
content: | bun build | Generate search index, then build for production |

title: Available Scripts
content: | bun start | Start production server on port 4000 |

title: Available Scripts
content: | bun lint | Run ESLint |

title: Available Scripts
content: | bun run generate-search-index | Build the full-text search index from MDX content |

title: Available Scripts
content: | bun run generate-git-metadata | Pre-generate Git last-modified metadata for all doc pages |

title: Available Scripts
content: Run bun run generate-git-metadata before your production build to display accurate last-modified dates and author information on each page.

title: Introduction
content: docs-template is a documentation site template built with Next.js, MDX, and Tailwind CSS. Write your docs in MDX files, configure navigation with a single JSON file, and get a fully-featured docs site — out of the box.

title: Features
content: MDX-based content — Write documentation in Markdown with full JSX component support.

title: Features
content: Internationalization (i18n) — Multi-locale support powered by next-intl, with per-locale MDX content and navigation.

title: Features
content: File-based routing — Pages map directly to src/docs/[locale]/[group]/[slug].mdx.

title: Features
content: JSON-driven navigation — Define the entire sidebar structure in src/docs/[locale]/navigation.json.

title: Features
content: Syntax highlighting — Powered by Shiki with copy-to-clipboard support.

title: Features
content: Full-text search — Client-side fuzzy search using Fuse.js.

title: Features
content: Table of contents — Auto-generated from headings in each MDX page.

title: Features
content: Dark / light mode — Theme toggling with system preference detection.

title: Features
content: Git metadata — Last modified date and author pulled from Git history.

title: Features
content: Responsive layout — Sidebar drawer for mobile, sticky sidebar for desktop.

title: Tech Stack
content: | Area | Library |

title: Tech Stack
content: | Framework | Next.js 16 (App Router) |

title: Tech Stack
content: | Styling | Tailwind CSS v4 |

title: Tech Stack
content: | MDX | @next/mdx |

title: Tech Stack
content: | i18n | next-intl |

title: Tech Stack
content: | Syntax highlighting | Shiki |

title: Tech Stack
content: | Search | Fuse.js |

title: Tech Stack
content: | Animations | Framer Motion |

title: Tech Stack
content: | Icons | Remixicon |

title: Tech Stack
content: | State management | Zustand |

title: Tech Stack
content: | Components | Radix UI |

title: Project Structure
content: The key directories you will interact with are:

title: Project Structure
content: Head to the Installation page to clone the repository and run the development server.

title: Adding Pages
content: Documentation pages in docs-template are MDX files stored under src/docs/[locale]/[group]/[slug].mdx. Adding a new page takes two steps: creating the MDX file and registering it in the navigation.

title: File Location
content: All MDX files follow this path convention:

title: File Location
content: <locale> — en or th (or any locale you have configured).

title: File Location
content: <group> — A folder that maps to a navigation group in the sidebar.

title: File Location
content: <slug> — The page identifier used in the URL.

title: File Location
content: A page at src/docs/en/guides/configuration.mdx is served at /en/docs/guides/configuration.

id: Adding Pages > Creating a Page > 1. Create the MDX file#p0
title: 1. Create the MDX file
content: Create a file at src/docs/[locale]/[group]/[slug].mdx. Every page must start with a metadata export:

id: Adding Pages > Creating a Page > 1. Create the MDX file#p1
title: 1. Create the MDX file
content: Always create an MDX file for each locale you support (e.g., both en and th) to avoid 404 errors when users switch languages.

id: Adding Pages > Creating a Page > 2. Register in navigation#p0
title: 2. Register in navigation
content: Open src/docs/[locale]/navigation.json for each locale and add a { "slug", "title" } entry to the relevant group's pages array:

id: Adding Pages > Creating a Page > 2. Register in navigation#p1
title: 2. Register in navigation
content: The page is now accessible in the sidebar.

id: Adding Pages > Adding a New Group#p0
title: Adding a New Group
content: If no existing group fits your page, add a new group object to the top-level array in both locale navigation files:

id: Adding Pages > Adding a New Group#p1
title: Adding a New Group
content: Page slugs must be unique within a group. Two pages with the same slug in the same group will cause routing conflicts.

title: MDX Components
content: docs-template maps standard Markdown elements to styled components automatically. A small set of additional components — like Callout — are available via import for richer content.

title: Auto-mapped Elements
content: The following elements require no import and are styled automatically:

title: Auto-mapped Elements
content: | Element | MDX syntax |

title: Auto-mapped Elements
content: | Paragraphs | Plain text |

title: Auto-mapped Elements
content: | Lists | - item / 1. item |

title: Auto-mapped Elements
content: | Tables | | col | col | |

title: Auto-mapped Elements
content: | Inline code | `code` |

title: Auto-mapped Elements
content: | Code blocks | ``` fenced blocks |

title: Auto-mapped Elements
content: | Links | [text](url) |

title: Auto-mapped Elements
content: | Dividers | --- |

title: Callout
content: Use Callout to highlight important information. Import it at the top of your MDX file:

title: Usage
content: The first child is rendered as the title; subsequent children are rendered as body content.

title: Types
content: | Type | When to use |

title: Types
content: | info | Setup notes, general context |

title: Types
content: | tip | Best practices, productivity hints |

title: Types
content: | warning | Potential issues, non-destructive cautions |

title: Types
content: | danger | Breaking changes, destructive or irreversible actions |

title: Types
content: Use this for setup notes or general context that readers should be aware of.

title: Types
content: Use this for best practices and recommendations.

title: Types
content: Use this to flag potential issues or configuration pitfalls.

title: Types
content: Use this for breaking changes or irreversible actions.

title: Collapsible callouts
content: Pass isCollapsed to render the callout collapsed by default:

title: Code Blocks
content: Fenced code blocks support syntax highlighting via Shiki. Add props directly on the opening fence line.

id: MDX Components > Code Blocks > Copy button#p0
title: Copy button
content: Add copy="true" to display a copy-to-clipboard button:

id: MDX Components > Code Blocks > File path label#p0
title: File path label
content: Append :path/to/file to the language identifier to show a file path header above the block:

id: MDX Components > Code Blocks > File path label#p1
title: File path label
content: The text after : is rendered as a label only — it does not affect syntax highlighting. Use the correct language identifier before : for proper highlighting.
